SEARCH
Package edu.yu.cs.com3810.device

Class InputDevice

java.lang.Object
edu.yu.cs.com3810.device.InputDevice
All Implemented Interfaces:
IEdgeDevice
Direct Known Subclasses:
Doorbell, LightSensor, MotionSensor
public abstract class InputDevice extends Object implements IEdgeDevice
Base class for input-type edge devices (motion sensors, light sensors, doorbells).

Input devices differ from output devices (lights, speakers) in that their primary role is to detect real-world events and publish them to the EVENT topic on the message bus. They do not typically receive commands, so executeCommand(Command) returns CommandStatus.INVALID_ACTION by default unless overridden.

Concrete subclasses implement getDeviceType() and call publishEvent(String, Map) whenever the physical sensor fires.

    Emits a heartbeat message to the HEARTBEAT topic on the message bus.
    void
    start(IMessageBus messageBus)
    Starts the device: registers with the message bus, publishes a RegistrationPayload to the REGISTER topic, subscribes to the COMMAND topic to receive commands, and begins emitting heartbeats on the HEARTBEAT topic.
    void
    stop()
    Gracefully shuts down the device, unsubscribing from the message bus and stopping heartbeat emission.

      protected final String deviceId
      The unique ID of this device.

    • name

      protected final String name
      The human-readable name of this device.

      The message bus used to publish events and heartbeats. Set during start(edu.yu.cs.com3810.messagebus.IMessageBus).

  • Constructor Details

    • InputDevice

      protected InputDevice(String deviceId, String name)
      Constructs an InputDevice.
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the unique ID for this device
      name - the human-readable name for this device

  • Method Details

    • getDeviceId

      public String getDeviceId()
      Returns the globally unique identifier of this device.
      Specified by:
      getDeviceId in interface IEdgeDevice
      Returns:
      device ID string

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns the human-readable name of this device.
      Specified by:
      getName in interface IEdgeDevice
      Returns:
      device name (e.g., "Kitchen Speaker")

    • getAvailableActions

      public List<DeviceAction> getAvailableActions()
      Input devices do not accept commands, so this returns an empty list. Subclasses may override if they support any actions.
      Specified by:
      getAvailableActions in interface IEdgeDevice
      Returns:
      an empty list

    • getDeviceType

      public abstract DeviceType getDeviceType()
      Returns the DeviceType for this specific input device. Implemented by each concrete subclass.
      Returns:
      the device type

    • start

      public void start(IMessageBus messageBus)
      Starts the device: registers with the message bus, publishes a RegistrationPayload to the REGISTER topic, subscribes to the COMMAND topic to receive commands, and begins emitting heartbeats on the HEARTBEAT topic.

      Registers with the message bus, publishes a RegistrationPayload to the REGISTER topic, and begins emitting periodic heartbeats.

      Specified by:
      start in interface IEdgeDevice
      Parameters:
      messageBus - the shared message bus to register and communicate through

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Gracefully shuts down the device, unsubscribing from the message bus and stopping heartbeat emission.
      Specified by:
      stop in interface IEdgeDevice

    • executeCommand

      public CommandStatus executeCommand(Command command)
      Input devices do not execute commands.
      Specified by:
      executeCommand in interface IEdgeDevice
      Parameters:
      command - the command received (ignored)
      Returns:
      always CommandStatus.INVALID_ACTION

    • sendHeartbeat

      public void sendHeartbeat()
      Emits a heartbeat message to the HEARTBEAT topic on the message bus. Called periodically by an internal timer so the coordinator knows the device is still online.
      Specified by:
      sendHeartbeat in interface IEdgeDevice

    • publishEvent

      protected void publishEvent(String eventType, Map<String,String> metadata)
      Constructs and publishes a DeviceEvent to the EVENT topic.

      Concrete subclasses call this whenever their physical sensor fires. The Automation Hub receives the event via its EVENT topic subscription and evaluates it against active rules.

      Parameters:
      eventType - a short string identifying the event (e.g., "MOTION_DETECTED")
      metadata - optional key-value metadata accompanying the event; may be empty
